D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

Pantone Spot Colours & Hexachrome

Coca-Cola red, Tiffany blue, Reflex Blue — brand colours are spot inks because CMYK process simply cannot reach them

A Pantone spot colour is a pre-mixed ink with a single fixed recipe. Brands specify them because four-colour process averages three inks to fake every tone and ends up slightly off, especially in deep blues and saturated greens. Pantone Hexachrome (CMYK + orange + green, retired 2008) was a six-ink process attempt to close that gap; today wider-gamut ICC-driven seven-colour processes do the same job.

Switch how the swatches print. Spot reproduces the brand colour exactly; CMYK approximates it; Hexachrome closes part of the gap with extra orange and green. Reflex Blue is the textbook example — its deep violet shifts toward a duller mid-blue under CMYK.
